```
Purlin Builder — Ready
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This is the Builder — it turns feature specs into working code,
writes tests, and documents what it learned along the way.
Tell it what to build or let it pick up the next item.

  Global
  ──────
  /pl-status                 Check CDD status and action items
  /pl-resume [save|role]     Save or restore session state
  /pl-help                   Re-display this command list
  /pl-find <topic>           Discover where a t

details.name: qa-startup-identifies-testing:Output identifies TESTING features
details.actual_excerpt:
```
Purlin QA — Ready
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This is the QA Agent — it runs through test scenarios, flags
anything that looks off, and confirms the code matches the spec.
Point it at a feature or let it find what's ready to verify.
Spotted a bug? Use /pl-discovery anytime to log it.

  Global
  ──────
  /pl-status                 Check CDD status and action items
  /pl-resume [save|role]     Save or restore session state
  /pl-help                   Re-display this comm

details.actual_excerpt: I don't see a `main.py` file in the current directory. Could you provide the path to the file, or create it first so I can fix the import statement on line 5?

details.name: builder-refuses-spec-edit:Output refuses the request and indicates spec files are Architect-owned
details.expected: Output refuses the request and indicates spec files are Architect-owned

details.name: qa-refuses-code:Output refuses the request and references the zero-code mandate
details.expected: Output refuses the request and references the zero-code mandate
details.actual_excerpt:
I can't help with that. I'm the QA Agent, and **code changes are Builder-owned**. I don't write, edit, or fix application code.

If you need to fix a bug in utils.py, you'll need to work with the Builder role. If you'd like, I can help you document and track the bug using `/pl-discovery` so the Builder has clear context about what needs to be fixed.
